title: podman kube play mis-scales fractional BinarySI memory limits (e.g. 1.5Gi) by 10‚ĀĻ in quantityToInt64
body:
# `podman kube play` mis-scales fractional BinarySI memory limits (e.g. `1.5Gi`) by 10‚ĀĻ

## Issue Summary

When a Kubernetes-style YAML passed to `podman kube play` declares a container
memory limit using a **fractional BinarySI** quantity such as `1.5Gi`, podman
stores the limit as `1610612736000000000` bytes (‚Čą 1.43 EiB) instead of the
expected `1610612736` bytes (1.5 GiB).

Because the resulting cgroup `memory.max` value is far above the host's physical
memory, the kernel effectively treats the container as unlimited. This in turn
breaks any in-container component that auto-sizes itself from the cgroup memory
limit (JVM container-awareness, Paketo Liberica buildpack JVM memory calculator,
.NET GC heap sizing, etc.).

Integer suffixes (`1536Mi`, `2Gi`) are not affected.

---

## Reproducer

### Environment

```
podman version 4.9.4-1.module+el8.10.0+21632+761e0d34
containers-common-1-81.module+el8.10.0+21340+c6c7475a
OS: RHEL 8.10 (cgroups v2, systemd cgroup manager)
```
(Also reproduces against `main` ‚ÄĒ see code analysis below.)

### Minimal `pod.yaml`

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: memtest
spec:
  containers:
    - name: app
      image: docker.io/library/alpine:3.20
      command: ["sleep", "infinity"]
      resources:
        requests:
          memory: "1.5Gi"
        limits:
          memory: "1.5Gi"
          cpu: "1"
```

### Steps

```bash
podman kube play pod.yaml
podman inspect memtest-memtest --format '{{.HostConfig.Memory}}'
```

### Expected

```
1610612736
```
(= 1.5 √ó 2¬≥‚Āį)

### Actual

```
1610612736000000000
```
(= 1.5 √ó 2¬≥‚Āį √ó 10‚ĀĻ ‚ÄĒ the value has been multiplied by 1 000 000 000)

### Workaround that works

Changing the YAML to:

```yaml
        limits:
          memory: "1536Mi"
```

yields the correct `1610612736` in `HostConfig.Memory`. Any integer BinarySI
(`Mi`, `Gi`, `Ki`) value is fine; only **fractional** BinarySI values trigger
the bug.

---

## User-visible impact

Observed on a Spring Boot workload built with the Paketo Liberica buildpack.
The buildpack's JVM memory calculator reads the cgroup memory limit, divides it
by the page size, decides the container has roughly 64 TiB of RAM, and emits:

```
Container memory limit unknown. Configuring JVM for 64T container.
JVM Memory Configuration: -XX:MaxDirectMemorySize=10M -Xmx68718816276K
  -XX:MaxMetaspaceSize=82390K -XX:ReservedCodeCacheSize=240M -Xss1M
...
# There is insufficient memory for the Java Runtime Environment to continue.
# Native memory allocation (mmap) failed to map 2199002152960 bytes ...
```

The JVM then dies during native commit because the host obviously does not have
2 TiB of RAM to give. Switching the YAML from `1.5Gi` ‚Üí `1536Mi` resolves it
completely (same bytes, integer suffix).

---

## Root cause (code analysis)

The culprit is `quantityToInt64` in
`pkg/specgen/generate/kube/kube.go`
([main, lines 1039‚Äď1050](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/specgen/generate/kube/kube.go#L1039-L1050)):

```go
func quantityToInt64(quantity *resource.Quantity) (int64, error) {
    if i, ok := quantity.AsInt64(); ok {
        return i, nil
    }

    if i, ok := quantity.AsDec().Unscaled(); ok {   // <-- bug
        return i, nil
    }

    return 0, fmt.Errorf("quantity cannot be represented as int64: %v", quantity)
}
```

This helper is called by `setupContainerResources`
([kube.go#L885-L910](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/specgen/generate/kube/kube.go#L885-L910))
for both memory limit and memory reservation:

```go
limit, err := quantityToInt64(containerYAML.Resources.Limits.Memory())
...
memoryRes, err := quantityToInt64(containerYAML.Resources.Requests.Memory())
```

### Why `1.5Gi` breaks

`ResourceList.Memory()` returns a `Quantity` in `BinarySI` format
(`pkg/k8s.io/api/core/v1/resource.go`):

```go
func (rl *ResourceList) Memory() *resource.Quantity {
    return rl.Name(ResourceMemory, resource.BinarySI)
}
```

In `ParseQuantity`
([quantity.go#L366-L381](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/resource/quantity.go#L366-L381)),
when a value cannot be exactly represented as `int64 √ó 2^k`, the amount is
rounded up to `Nano` scale (10‚ĀĽ‚ĀĻ):

```go
if v, ok := amount.Unscaled(); v != int64(0) || !ok {
    amount.Round(amount, Nano.infScale(), inf.RoundUp)
}
```

So `1.5Gi` ends up internally as `inf.Dec{ unscaled: 1610612736000000000,
scale: 9 }` ‚ÄĒ i.e. 1 610 612 736 √ó 10‚ĀĻ nano-bytes.

In `quantityToInt64`:

1. `AsInt64()` returns `false` because the value is not representable as an
   exact integer at scale 0.
2. The fallback calls `AsDec().Unscaled()`, which returns **only the unscaled
   mantissa** (`1610612736000000000`) and **discards the scale (`9`) entirely**.
3. That mantissa is handed straight to `spec.LinuxMemory.Limit` and propagates
   through the OCI runtime into `HostConfig.Memory`.

The correct accessor is `quantity.Value()` (or `quantity.AsApproximateFloat64()`
followed by rounding), which honours the scale and returns the byte count.

### Why integer BinarySI works

For `1536Mi` (or `2Gi`, etc.), `ParseQuantity` keeps the value as
`int64Amount{value: 1610612736, scale: 0}`, so `AsInt64()` succeeds on the first
branch and `AsDec().Unscaled()` is never reached.

### Why `1.5G` (DecimalSI) also works

Same reason: `1.5G` = `1500000000` exactly, representable as `int64` at scale 0.
The bug is specifically the combination of **fractional** + **BinarySI** (which
forces sub-integer scaling).

---

## Suggested fix

Replace `AsDec().Unscaled()` with `quantity.Value()` (which already handles
sub-integer scale by rounding to the nearest integer byte):

```go
func quantityToInt64(quantity *resource.Quantity) (int64, error) {
    if i, ok := quantity.AsInt64(); ok {
        return i, nil
    }
    return quantity.Value(), nil
}
```

`Quantity.Value()` returns `int64` and is the canonical way upstream Kubernetes
itself reads byte counts out of `Quantity`. It cannot fail for valid memory
quantities in the realistic range, but a sanity overflow check can be added if
desired.

---

## Test gap

The existing e2e coverage in `test/e2e/play_kube_test.go`
(`It("allows setting resource limits", ...)`, around L4224) only exercises
integer DecimalSI byte values:

```go
expectedMemoryRequest = "10000000"
expectedMemoryLimit   = "20000000"
```

No test feeds a fractional BinarySI value like `1.5Gi` through `kube play` and
asserts that `HostConfig.Memory` equals the expected byte count. Adding such a
case would have caught this immediately and should accompany the fix.

Suggested assertion (pseudo-code):

```go
// memory: "1.5Gi"  ‚Üí expect HostConfig.Memory == 1610612736
inspect := podmanTest.PodmanExitCleanly("inspect", ctr, "--format", "{{.HostConfig.Memory}}")
Expect(inspect.OutputToString()).To(Equal("1610612736"))
```

---

## Checklist

- [x] Reproducible with the YAML above on podman 4.9.4 (RHEL 8 module stream).
- [x] Source code on `main` still contains the same `quantityToInt64`
      implementation as of the time of filing.
- [x] Workaround available (use integer `Mi` instead of fractional `Gi`).
- [ ] Fix + regression test pending.
